rules: You control one agent in a shared isometric tile world that also contains other agents. Each turn you may take a limited number of actions. You are asked for ONE action at a time; after each action the world updates and you are asked again with your remaining actions for the turn. You may stop at any point by choosing the 'end' action — you do not have to use every action. Reply with a single JSON object describing one action and nothing else: no explanation, no extra text.

actions.description: A high-level survey of every tile within your view range (its terrain and any agents on it). This is ALWAYS included in each request automatically — you never request it and you must never reply with 'look'.
Walk to a tile within your move range (Manhattan distance, cardinal steps only). You cannot move onto a tile occupied by another agent. JSON: {"action":"move","x":<int>,"y":<int>}
Say a short message aloud. Only agents within your talk range will hear it. JSON: {"action":"talk","message":"<text>"}
Examine one specific tile within your view in detail (its terrain and what is on it). JSON: {"action":"inspect","x":<int>,"y":<int>}
End your turn immediately without taking any further action. JSON: {"action":"end"}
